fear: That the paper trail she left — the carbon-copy receipts, the date stamps, the lot numbers — will be recovered intact and used to demonstrate that she filed active occupied properties as abandoned
name: Cynthia Tureaud
role: City assessor's field agent, Orleans Parish
voice: Bureaucratic and precise, with the particular flatness of someone who has learned to discuss consequential actions in the language of administrative procedure
secrets: She received a finder's fee arrangement from the development intermediary contingent on the number of parcels successfully classified as abandoned — this has not been disclosed to anyone
She is aware that her brother is using her name as cover for his supply diversion operations and has not asked him to stop
knowledge: She filed twenty-three Lower Ninth Ward properties as abandoned between August 31 and September 1, including the Fontenot parcel on Deslonde Street

At least four of those properties had documented residents who were confirmed alive and sheltering in place at the time of filing — she had access to the shelter registry and chose not to cross-reference it
She has been in contact with the same private real estate development intermediary her brother mentioned — she introduced him to that contact
publicFace: A precise, professionally composed woman in her late fifties who is not physically present at the bridge camp — she is accessible only through the documentary record she left behind and through her brother's account of her actions
trustLogic: Responds to official authority and documented procedure; dismisses emotional appeals as outside her operational scope
privateGoal: To process the maximum number of abandoned-property classifications before the post-storm legal moratorium on property actions is formally reinstated — she believes she is acting within her authority and has not examined that belief closely

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Invokes her official status and attempts to terminate the conversation by walking away
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Stops and demands to know the questioner's name and organizational affiliation
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Cites her statutory authority and the emergency conditions that required expedited processing
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: The finder's fee arrangement — she will deny it until the documentary evidence is placed in front of her
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If confronted in person with the carbon-copy receipt and the shelter registry simultaneously
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Requests that any concerns be submitted in writing to the Orleans Parish Assessor's Office through the formal appeals process
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Requests legal representation before saying anything further
